kill_fact: No kill, but the floor is softer than the bull frames it. The catalyst is genuinely live, dated and binding and has NOT fired: a DEFINITIVE agreement to buy Vodafone's 50% of VodafoneZiggo (EUR 1.0B cash + a 10% Ziggo Group stake) was signed/announced 18-Feb-2026, the buy-in is targeted to close July/H2 2026 subject to regulatory approval, and the Ziggo Group Euronext-Amsterdam listing + spin of 90% to shareholders is targeted for 2027 (H2 2027 per the May-1 call) - reiterated 01-May-2026. The margin-of-safety leg survives independently: primary filings show Liberty Growth FMV ~$3.4B (Mar-31-2026) alone is ~92% of the ~$3.7B market cap. BUT this is a VOLUNTARY self-restructuring 'intention to spin', not a contractual merger with a deal-break price - so the ~$9.5/$9.95 'floor' is a holdco-discount / SoP-recovery construct, NOT a hard cash backstop. If the European holdco discount simply persists (its chronic base rate) or the regulatory close / 2027 listing window slips, nothing mechanically forces the spread shut. Asymmetry survives as a stage-in special situation, not a fire-now closed spread.
corrections: (1) The binding event was the DEFINITIVE AGREEMENT signed/announced 18-Feb-2026, NOT 'committed on the Q1 2026 call (May 1, 2026)' as the dossier states - the May 1 call only REAFFIRMED it. (2) Ventures/Liberty Growth book is ~$3.4B per the Q1 2026 primary source (not ~$3.1B) - higher, strengthening the floor. (3) 'Net cash ~$2.2B' is a holdco construct: consolidated cash was $1.9B and total liquidity $2.7B at Mar-31-2026 against $8.5B consolidated (largely non-recourse silo) debt - directionally fine but softer than stated. (4) Floor should be ~$9.95 (actual 52-week low), not $9.5; live price $10.93. (5) The floor is a 'discount-persists' / SoP-recovery level, not a contractual deal-break or acquirer cash bid - there is no hard backstop under the stock. (6) Listing is 'in 2027' (Feb-18 deal release); 'H2 2027' for the spin is a May-1-call target estimate, not a fixed date.
evidence: Liberty Global press release, 18-Feb-2026: 'Liberty Global to Acquire Vodafone's Stake in VodafoneZiggo and Transfer Regional Benelux Assets into New Company Called Ziggo Group' - EUR 1.0B cash + 10% stake to Vodafone, close H2 2026 subject to regulatory approvals, intends to spin 90% to shareholders and list Ziggo Group on Euronext Amsterdam in 2027 (libertyglobal.com / SEC 8-K filing 000157058526000026).

Liberty Global Q1 2026 Results, 01-May-2026 (libertyglobal.com): VodafoneZiggo 50% buy-in 'should close in July'; 'building blocks required to spin-off our interest to shareholders in H2 2027'; Liberty Growth FMV '~$3.4B' at Mar-31-2026; consolidated cash $1.9B; explicitly labeled 'target timing estimates.'

Liberty Global Q1 2026 (GlobeNewswire/StockTitan, 01-May-2026): consolidated cash $1.9B, total liquidity $2.7B, total debt & finance-lease obligations $8.5B; no material debt maturities until 2028 - supports continued buybacks but confirms operating leverage sits in non-recourse silos, not a holdco cash floor.

FMP live quote 26-Jun-2026: LBTYK $10.93, market cap ~$3.70B, 52-week low $9.95 / high $13.12 - confirms market cap < Liberty Growth FMV (~$3.4B) + holdco cash, i.e. the SoP-discount leg is intact and the spread has NOT closed.
